title: TCP connection to willselfdestruct.com confirmed
finding: A TCP stream in nitroba.pcap shows a connection originating from the dorm room network to willselfdestruct.com. The HTTP payload content of this stream matches the harassing message subsequently received by victim Lily Tuckrige.
supporting_evidence: E-001 nitroba.pcap — Follow TCP Stream in Wireshark
impact_or_relevance: Directly links the dorm room network to the delivery of the harassing message via the ephemeral messaging service.
alternative_explanation: No credible alternative — payload content matches victim's received message.

title: Gmail session reveals authenticated account jcoachj@gmail.com
finding: Traffic following the culprit source IP includes a Gmail HTTP session in which the Google server responded with the string 'Loading jcoachj@gmail.com…', indicating an authenticated session for that account was active on the device at the time of the harassment.
supporting_evidence: E-001 nitroba.pcap — HTTP stream inspection in Wireshark
impact_or_relevance: Ties the network activity to a specific authenticated Google identity.
alternative_explanation: A third party could have used the open Wi-Fi; without endpoint forensics device-level attribution cannot be absolute.

title: Johnny Coach identified as holder of jcoachj@gmail.com
finding: Cross-referencing the Gmail account jcoachj@gmail.com against the Chemistry 109 summer course roster provided by Nitroba University identifies the account holder as student Johnny Coach, enrolled in Lily Tuckrige's class.
impact_or_relevance: Attributes the harassing email campaign to a specific identified student in the victim's class.
alternative_explanation: Account name alone is circumstantial without corroboration — corroborated here by the email content match (F-001) and class enrollment.
